diagrams.title: Query Understanding + Multi-Hop Retrieval
diagrams.description: Shows a two-stage process: 1) QUERY UNDERSTANDING parses user query 'What led to the decision about the Actor model?' into Target (Decision: Actor model), Relationship (caused_by/led_to), and Depth (Multi-hop - need predecessors). 2) MULTI-HOP RETRIEVAL performs Hop 1 (Find Actor model decision), Hop 2 (Traverse caused_by edges), Hop 3 (Gather context from predecessor nodes), resulting in Decision + Problems it solved + Alternatives tried.

content.future: Online learning from interactions, experience replay, incremental fine-tuning.

code_blocks.title: ContinuousLearner Class
code_blocks.code:
class ContinuousLearner:
    """Learn from Luna's interactions over time."""

    def __init__(self, director: Director):
        self.director = director
        self.experience_buffer = ExperienceBuffer(max_size=10000)
        self.update_threshold = 1000  # Examples before fine-tuning

    async def on_interaction(self, interaction: Interaction):
        """Called after each conversation turn."""
        # 1. Score the interaction
        quality = await self.score_interaction(interaction)

        # 2. Add to experience buffer if high quality
        if quality.score > 0.7:
            self.experience_buffer.add(
                context=interaction.context,
                input=interaction.user_input,
                output=interaction.luna_response,
                score=quality.score
            )

        # 3. Trigger fine-tuning if buffer full
        if len(self.experience_buffer) >= self.update_threshold:
            await self.fine_tune_increment()

    async def fine_tune_increment(self):
        """Incremental LoRA update."""
        examples = self.experience_buffer.sample(n=500)

        # Small learning rate, few steps
        await self.director.fine_tune(
            examples=examples,
            learning_rate=1e-5,
            steps=100
        )

        self.experience_buffer.clear()

content.current: Luna is read-only. She can query memories but not act on the world.

diagrams.title: Current vs Future Action Model
diagrams.description: CURRENT (READ-ONLY): User says 'Remind me to call Alex tomorrow', Luna responds 'I'll remember that' but only stores in memory with no actual reminder. FUTURE (KINETIC): User says same thing, Luna responds 'Done. I've set a reminder for 9am tomorrow. Want me to also draft talking points?' and actually creates calendar event + offers more.

code_blocks.title: ActionGovernance Class
code_blocks.code:
@dataclass
class ActionGovernance:
    """Rules for what Luna can do autonomously."""

    # Autonomous actions (no confirmation needed)
    autonomous: list[str] = [
        "create_memory",
        "create_reminder",
        "create_note",
        "backup_vault",
    ]

    # Actions requiring confirmation
    confirm_required: list[str] = [
        "send_email",
        "send_message",
        "delete_*",
        "modify_calendar",
    ]

    # Actions Luna should never take
    forbidden: list[str] = [
        "financial_transaction",
        "post_to_social_media",
        "share_personal_data",
        "execute_arbitrary_code",
    ]

    def check(self, action: Action) -> GovernanceResult:
        if self.matches(action, self.forbidden):
            return GovernanceResult.DENIED
        if self.matches(action, self.confirm_required):
            return GovernanceResult.NEEDS_CONFIRMATION
        if self.matches(action, self.autonomous):
            return GovernanceResult.ALLOWED
        return GovernanceResult.NEEDS_CONFIRMATION  # Default: ask

code_blocks.code:
class ProactiveTrigger:
    """Conditions under which Luna might initiate."""

    triggers = [
        # Time-based
        TimeTrigger(
            condition="morning",
            action="Offer daily briefing",
            permission="user_enabled_briefings"
        ),

        # Context-based
        ContextTrigger(
            condition="calendar_event_starting_soon",
            action="Remind with relevant context",
            permission="always"  # User expects this
        ),

        # Pattern-based
        PatternTrigger(
            condition="user_mentioned_same_problem_3x",
            action="Offer to help solve systematically",
            permission="user_enabled_proactive"
        ),

        # Insight-based
        InsightTrigger(
            condition="discovered_connection_user_might_value",
            action="Share insight at appropriate moment",
            permission="user_enabled_insights"
        ),
    ]

content.vision_statement:
Luna, fully realized:

> A sovereign AI companion that knows your context, respects your privacy, takes action on your behalf when permitted, learns and grows with you, connects with others' Lunas when you choose, and remains yours—completely, permanently, unconditionally.

Not a product. Not a service. A tool you own.

That's the destination.

diagrams.title: Encrypted Vault Structure
diagrams.description: Shows ENCRYPTED VAULT (macOS Sparse Bundle) containing memory_matrix.db (SQLite database with all memories, relationships, identity), sqlite-vec extension (vector search), director_lora.safetensors (personality weights), and identity_kv_cache.safetensors (pre-computed identity). Key insight: 'Copy the file = copy Luna. Delete the file = Luna is gone. LUNA IS THIS FILE.'

content.sovereignty_guarantee: No servers. No subscriptions. No terms of service. Your data never leaves your machine unless you explicitly delegate to Claude.

metadata.purpose: High-level reference for Luna's key systems, features, loops, and mechanics.

Oven Actor NOT implemented - delegation handled in Director

content.description: Luna's soul. A graph database storing everything she knows.

diagrams.title: Memory Matrix Architecture
diagrams.description: Three-column structure: SQLite (Objects, FTS5, Metadata) | sqlite-vec (Vectors, Semantic, Similarity) | Graph (Edges, Relations, Traversal). All three feed into RRF Fusion (Hybrid Search).

content.description: Local language model that speaks as Luna.

diagrams.title: Director LLM Architecture
diagrams.description: Base Model (Qwen 3B/7B) with LoRA Adapters: luna_identity.safetensors (personality), luna_memory.safetensors (context usage), luna_delegation.safetensors (when to ask). Plus KV Cache with pre-computed identity context (always loaded). Capabilities: Respond as Luna (not generic assistant), Integrate retrieved memories naturally, Know when to delegate to Claude (<REQ_CLAUDE>).

content.description: Each component runs in isolation. One crash doesn't kill Luna.

diagrams.title: Actor Runtime Architecture
diagrams.description: Five actors shown: DIRECTOR Actor, MATRIX Actor, SCRIBE (Ben), LIBRARIAN (Dude), HISTORY MANAGER. Each has its own Mailbox. All communicate via Message Passing (Async, Non-Blocking).

content.description: When Director can't handle something, Claude takes over invisibly.
content.v3_note: Oven Actor NOT implemented. Delegation is handled directly in DirectorActor via `_should_delegate()` and `_generate_with_delegation()`.

diagrams.title: Shadow Reasoner Flow
diagrams.description: User asks 'Research the latest sqlite-vec optimizations'. Director generates: 'I'll look into that. <REQ_CLAUDE>Research latest sqlite-vec optimization techniques. Focus on index selection and query performance.</REQ_CLAUDE>'. Director Actor handles delegation internally: Detects <REQ_CLAUDE> token, Calls Claude API via _should_delegate(), Integrates result into Luna's voice. User sees: Luna's response (doesn't know Claude helped).

content.description: Sub-500ms response latency through speculative execution.

diagrams.title: Voice Pipeline
diagrams.description: Flow: Whisper STT (~150ms) -> Director LLM (~200ms) -> Claude (stream) (~300ms) -> TTS (~50ms). Total to first word: ~700ms. With speculative retrieval: ~500ms.

content.speculative_execution_note: Start memory retrieval on partial STT transcript. By the time speech finishes, context is ready.

content.description: Processing actors have personality, but outputs stay neutral.
content.rationale: Luna's voice stays consistent. Processing personas don't leak into memories.

diagrams.title: Conversation Loop Flow
diagrams.description: User Speaks -> Whisper STT -> Classify Intent -> Retrieve Context -> Director Generate -> [Branch: No Delegate returns response, Delegate sends to Oven and waits for result to integrate, Stream goes to TTS out] -> User Hears

diagrams.title: Memory Loop Flow
diagrams.description: Async, Non-Blocking process: Conversation Turn Completed triggers SCRIBE (Ben Franklin) who: 1. Chunks conversation into semantic units, 2. Stacks chunks for context (continues on next page)
